title: Fab Inspector Rules File
description: Schema for Fab Inspector rule files. Each file contains a 'rules' array of inspection rule objects that validate Fabric items using JSONLogic expressions.

$schema.description: Optional JSON Schema reference for IDE validation.

rule.description: A single Fab Inspector inspection rule.

id.description: Unique identifier for the rule (e.g. "CHECK_THEME_NAME"). Used for referencing in output and CI/CD.

name.description: Human-readable name displayed in results, console output, and reports.

description.description: Detailed explanation of what the rule validates and why.

disabled.description: When true, the rule is skipped during execution.

logType.description: Severity level for rule violations. Default: "warning".

itemType.description: Fabric item type(s) this rule targets. Use '|' to combine multiple types (e.g. "DataPipeline|VariableLibrary"). Use "*" for cross-item rules, "none" for API-only rules.

part.description: Part iterator selector. For Report itemType: a reserved part name (Report, ReportExtensions, Pages, PagesHeader, AllPages, Visuals, AllVisuals, MobileVisuals, AllMobileVisuals, Bookmarks, BookmarksHeader, AllBookmarks, Files). For other item types: a regex pattern matching file paths (folder separators normalised to ':').

test.description: Test definition. A 2-element array [logic, expectedResult] or a 3-element array [logic, dataMapping, expectedResult]. The logic is a JSONLogic expression. The optional dataMapping binds named variables via JSON Pointer paths (prefixed with '/'). The expectedResult is the value the logic must produce for the rule to pass.

oneOf.description: 3-element test: [logic, dataMapping, expectedResult] — with data mapping.

applyPatch.description: When true and a 'patch' is defined, the patch will be applied automatically to fix failing items.

patch.description: Auto-fix patch definition. A 2-element array: [targetPart, patchOperations]. Only Report parts are currently supported.

prefixItems.description: Array of RFC 6902 JSON Patch operations.

pathErrorWhenNoMatch.description: When true, raises an error if the part iterator doesn't match any files. Useful for validating expected item structure.

dataMapping.description: Data mapping object. Keys are variable names accessible via {"var": "name"} in the logic expression. Values are JSON Pointer paths (prefixed with '/') into the current part content, or literal values.

expectedResult.description: The expected result of the logic expression. The rule passes when the logic output matches this value. Common patterns: true/false for boolean assertions, [] for 'no violations found' (filter-based rules), or a specific value for exact match.

patchTarget.description: The Report part to apply the patch against.

jsonPatchOperation.description: A single RFC 6902 JSON Patch operation.

oneOf.description: Add a value at the specified path.

path.description: JSON Pointer path where the value will be added.

oneOf.description: Remove the value at the specified path.

path.description: JSON Pointer path of the value to remove.

oneOf.description: Replace the value at the specified path.

path.description: JSON Pointer path of the value to replace.

oneOf.description: Move a value from one path to another.

from.description: JSON Pointer path of the source value.

path.description: JSON Pointer path of the destination.

oneOf.description: Copy a value from one path to another.

from.description: JSON Pointer path of the source value.

path.description: JSON Pointer path of the destination.

oneOf.description: Test (assert) that a value at the specified path equals the given value.
